timeline_html:
Summary. Lisa’s shedding began about two months ago and has since reached a steady plateau. Several overlapping factors—from hormonal changes to sustained stress—appear to have contributed.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Surgery or hospitalization (resolved; date not provided)
  • Started or stopped birth control / hormonal treatment (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Menopause or perimenopause symptoms (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started date not provided)
  • Major injury, blood loss, accident (resolved; date not provided)
  • Chronic illness flare (ongoing; started date not provided)

Timeline forecast. Based on the typical lag after hormonal or medical stressors, shedding likely peaked around February–March 2026 and should gradually calm by May–June 2026, with baby hairs starting to show around that time. Because ongoing stress and hormonal shifts persist, full stabilization may take a bit longer. Dominant driver: chronic stress and perimenopause, as both are still active and align with the timing of your current plateau.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization phase—shedding has leveled, and early regrowth is beginning. Keep supporting your body and stress balance; the groundwork for stronger new strands is already in motion. 🌱
